articles.title: China blocks Meta from acquiring AI startup Manus
articles.summary: Meta said Monday that the transaction "complied fully with applicable law" and that it anticipates "an appropriate resolution to the inquiry."

articles.title: Pompeii archaeologists use AI to reconstruct man killed in volcano's eruption
articles.summary: Archaeologists have used AI for the first time to digitally reconstruct the face of a man killed in the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius, offering a new way to understand one of history's most famous

articles.title: How China is responding to the stalling of talks between the U.S. and Iran
articles.summary: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East

articles.title: Trump Is Dissatisfied With Iran’s Plan to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The proposal would have set aside questions about what to do with Iran’s nuclear program.

articles.title: South Carolina's measles outbreak is over. But more are brewing around the country
articles.summary: The virus infected nearly 1,000 people in the state before the state declared it over. Meanwhile, cases are spreading across many parts of the U.S., with more than 20 outbreaks currently active.

articles.title: Investigators in Trump Assassination Attempt Look Through Bluesky for Suspect’s Motive
articles.summary: Federal authorities are looking into whether Cole Tomas Allen posted on Bluesky as “coldforce,” who wrote and promoted liberal views that did not stand out on the left-leaning platform.

articles.title: Republicans Push for Trump’s White House Ballroom After Gala Attack
articles.summary: The attack on a press dinner in Washington, which is being called an attempted assassination of President Trump, has also renewed the fight over reopening the Homeland Security Department.

articles.title: Charges Against Suspect in Trump Assassination Attempt Based on Shotgun Shell and a Screed
articles.summary: The authorities say the California man stormed a black-tie gala on Saturday seeking to kill the president.

articles.title: Ron DeSantis Aims to Add Four Republican House Seats in Florida Redistricting Push
articles.summary: The Republican-controlled Legislature is meeting in Tallahassee this week to vote on the map, which would apply for the 2026 midterms if passed.

articles.title: Supreme Court heard case on how to label risks of popular weed killer
articles.summary: How the Supreme Court rules could have implications for tens of thousands of lawsuits against Roundup maker Monsanto, which is now owned by Bayer.

articles.title: Privacy and law enforcement clash as the Supreme Court wrestles with 'geofence' warrants
articles.summary: In oral arguments at the Supreme Court Monday, most of the justices aimed pointed questions at both sides, with the usual conservative-liberal alignments scrambled like an egg.

articles.title: President and First Lady Melania Trump Demand ABC Fire Jimmy Kimmel Over ‘Widow’ Joke
articles.summary: The joke was recorded two days before the White House correspondents’ dinner, where a gunman tried to storm the press gala.

articles.title: Sergey Brin Moves to the Right, With a ‘MAGA Girlfriend’ by His Side
articles.summary: After once backing liberal causes, the Google co-founder has praised President Trump, donated to Republicans and spent $57 million to try to block a California billionaire tax.

articles.title: Here's a look inside security at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner
articles.summary: Saturday's shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner raised questions about how close the alleged gunman got to the president and what the Secret Service security looked like.

articles.title: Melania Trump wants ABC to 'take a stand' against Jimmy Kimmel after 'hateful' joke
articles.summary: Two days before the White House Correspondents' Dinner ended in gunfire, Kimmel delivered a mock Correspondents' Dinner speech during a sketch on his show. The first lady said it was "corrosive."

articles.title: Virginia Court Weighs Legality of New Redistricting Map Approved Last Week
articles.summary: Oral arguments on Monday morning lasted about an hour. It was not clear how justices would rule.

articles.title: Florida's DeSantis unveils a voting map that could add to Trump's GOP redistricting
articles.summary: Florida's governor has called lawmakers to meet starting Tuesday. They'll consider a fast-track redistricting that could flip some House seats held by Democrats to Republicans.

articles.title: Alleged Correspondents' Dinner shooter to appear in court. And, Charles III visits U.S.
articles.summary: The suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident is set to appear in federal court today. And, King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington today for a state visit.

articles.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy continues in Russia, as Trump reviews Iran's latest proposal
articles.summary: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

articles.title: East Africa redefines marathon limits as Sabastian Sawe leads historic charge
articles.summary: East Africa has rewritten marathon history as Sabastian Sawe produced a stunning breakthrough at the London Marathon, redefining what was thought possible over the marathon distance.

articles.title: Palestinian officials hail local elections in a Gaza community and the West Bank
articles.summary: Palestinian officials say local elections in Gaza and the West Bank mark a step toward a long-delayed presidential election. The Palestinian Authority hasn't held a presidential election in 21 years.

articles.title: Egypt's Grand Museum opens after many delays
articles.summary: We visit the new Grand Egyptian Museum, a spacious museum outside Cairo dedicated to displaying artifacts from ancient Egypt.  It has finally opened its doors after decades of planning and constructio

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
